extraction.one_line_summary: LAMPAERT Yves won the 13km Copenhagen TT in 15:17, beating VAN AERT Wout by 5 seconds after BISSEGGER Stefan crashed twice early.
tactical_patterns: BISSEGGER crashed twice in opening kilometers chasing yellow, demonstrating aggression in early corners costs time beyond immediate losses.
LAMPAERT took more corner risk than VAN AERT in wet conditions, suggesting winning margin came from technical handling not power.
PIDCOCK placed third ahead of world champion GANNA, signaling first Tour TT aggression reveals form ambitions beyond mountain domestique role.

rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: positive: Won after Roubaix spectator crash and Belgian TT silver to Evenepoel shows mental resilience under pressure
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: negative: Double crash chasing yellow reveals overcommitment in wet technical sections sacrifices realistic GC time gains
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: Lost by 5 seconds despite similar conditions suggests conservative cornering prioritized safety over stage win
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: positive: Third place 16 seconds down in first Tour TT signals genuine stage-hunting ambition beyond mountain support
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: negative: Fourth behind PIDCOCK despite world champion status indicates flat short TT form weaker than expected
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: Sixth place flags-for-Denmark ride met home expectations without threatening podium
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: Seventh limits losses but finishing behind PEDERSEN suggests TT still relative GC weakness
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Placed near VINGEGAARD confirms both GC favorites conceded similar time to TT specialists
rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: neutral: Tenth after Dutch TT championship win shows national form translated but insufficient for stage podium
